entry.text: The precinct house squats at the intersection like a bunker that lost its war. The front desk is still manned — sort of. A skeleton in uniform slumps behind the bulletproof partition, ID badge dangling from a lanyard around what remains of its neck. The armory door at the back of the lobby is ajar, its electronic lock hanging off the frame by a single wire.

choices.label: Bypass the lock and get into the armory
Search the bullpen desks and lockers

bullpen.text: The bullpen is a ruin of overturned desks and faded paper. Someone has been living here — a nest of scorched foam and filthy cloth sits between two filing cabinets, still warm. Lockers line the far wall, most forced open, but a few still hold their standard-issue contents.

choices.label: Follow the warm nest trail to the armory

armory.text: The armory is a {{state}}. Most racks are stripped bare, but the evidence cage in the corner was overlooked — padlocked shut, tagged parcels still inside, a few sealed MRE bags someone confiscated and never logged out. You work through the cage methodically, taking what hasn't turned to dust.

lock_shock.text: The dangling wire is live — a capacitor discharge from a backup battery slams through you when your tool makes contact. Your teeth ring for ten minutes after.

squatter_ambush.text: Whoever nested in that bullpen comes back before you finish. A thrown chunk of concrete catches you across the temple before you can retreat to cover.

resident_attack.text: The nest's owner — gaunt, feverish, armed with a fire extinguisher — is already inside the armory and charges the moment you enter. You take a hard blow across the ribs before you can break away.
